title: festuca rubra subsp. bartherei
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. barthei, also known as red fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. barthei include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Festuca rubra subsp. barthei typically involve infusion or decoction of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application.
